{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Neo-Indian Attack| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|= | | | | |pd|nd| | |= | | | | | | |bl| |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl| |ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Neo-Indian Attack= The pinning of the f6 knight looks similar to the Torre Attack, but here White cannot force the doubling of pawns. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}.
